Wisconsin 2026 Introduced S.B.778
Introduced
Establishes a comprehensive packaging reduction and recycling program. Requires producers to reduce packaging. Prohibits the use of certain toxic substances in packaging including phthalates, bisphenols, PFAS, halogenated flame retardants, formaldehyde, and more.
 Establishes a comprehensive packaging reduction and recycling program. Requires producers to reduce packaging. Prohibits the use of certain toxic substances in packaging including phthalates, bisphenols, PFAS, halogenated flame retardants, formaldehyde, and more.
Personal Care Products 44 bills
State Year Status Bill Notes
California 2024 Adopted A.B.2515
Adopted
Prohibits any person from manufacturing, distributing, selling, or offering for sale in the state any menstrual products that contain regulated PFAS.
 Prohibits any person from manufacturing, distributing, selling, or offering for sale in the state any menstrual products that contain regulated PFAS.
California 2022 Adopted AB 2771
Adopted
Restricts perfluoroalkyl and polyfluoroalkyl substances in cosmetic products. Effective January 1, 2025.
 Restricts perfluoroalkyl and polyfluoroalkyl substances in cosmetic products. Effective January 1, 2025.
California 2020 Adopted SB 312
Adopted
Requires cosmetics manufacturers to disclose lists of chemicals, flavors, and fragrances included in their products to the state along with associated health hazards. Requires the state to maintain a public website displaying the information.
 Requires cosmetics manufacturers to disclose lists of chemicals, flavors, and fragrances included in their products to the state along with associated health hazards. Requires the state to maintain a public website displaying the information.
California 2020 Adopted AB 2762
Adopted
Bans select chemicals in cosmetic products (specific phthalates, parabens, phenylenediamines, formaldehyde, mercury, and 13 PFAS).
 Bans select chemicals in cosmetic products (specific phthalates, parabens, phenylenediamines, formaldehyde, mercury, and 13 PFAS).
Colorado 2024 Adopted S.B.24-081
Adopted
Prohibits the sale or distribution of certain outdoor apparel intended for extreme or extended use in severe wet conditions that contain intentionally added PFAS chemicals. Prohibits PFAS in cleaning products, cookware, dental floss, menstruation products, ski wax, and textile articles. Prohibits a person from installing artificial turf that contains intentionally added PFAS chemicals on state property.
 Prohibits the sale or distribution of certain outdoor apparel intended for extreme or extended use in severe wet conditions that contain intentionally added PFAS chemicals. Prohibits PFAS in cleaning products, cookware, dental floss, menstruation products, ski wax, and textile articles. Prohibits a person from installing artificial turf that contains intentionally added PFAS chemicals on state property.
Colorado 2022 Adopted HB 1345
Adopted
Restricts the sale and distribution of consumer products containing PFAS chemicals. Consumer products include oil and gas products; carpets or rugs; cosmetics; fabric treatments; food packaging; juvenile products; textile furnishings; and upholstered furniture.
 Restricts the sale and distribution of consumer products containing PFAS chemicals. Consumer products include oil and gas products; carpets or rugs; cosmetics; fabric treatments; food packaging; juvenile products; textile furnishings; and upholstered furniture.
Connecticut 2024 Adopted S.B.292
Adopted
Prohibits the sale or distribution of the following products containing intentionally added PFAS: apparel, carpets or rugs, cleaning products, cookware, cosmetics, dental floss, fabric treatments, children’s products, menstruation products, textile furnishings, ski wax, and upholstered furniture. Requires manufacturers to submit information on products containing intentionally added PFAS to the state.
 Prohibits the sale or distribution of the following products containing intentionally added PFAS: apparel, carpets or rugs, cleaning products, cookware, cosmetics, dental floss, fabric treatments, children’s products, menstruation products, textile furnishings, ski wax, and upholstered furniture. Requires manufacturers to submit information on products containing intentionally added PFAS to the state.
Georgia 2026 Introduced H.B.1143
Introduced
Requires disclosure of all ingredients in menstrual products. Prohibits certain substances in menstrual products including lead, mercury, formaldehyde, triclosan, phthalates, and PFAS.
 Requires disclosure of all ingredients in menstrual products. Prohibits certain substances in menstrual products including lead, mercury, formaldehyde, triclosan, phthalates, and PFAS.
Illinois 2025 Introduced S.B.0117
Introduced
Prohibits certain products containing intentionally added PFAS, including cookware, cosmetics, dental floss, juvenile products, menstrual products, intimate apparel, and food packaging or food contact products.
 Prohibits certain products containing intentionally added PFAS, including cookware, cosmetics, dental floss, juvenile products, menstrual products, intimate apparel, and food packaging or food contact products.
Illinois 2025 Introduced H.B.3409
Introduced
Prohibits the sale and manufacture of cosmetics with certain harmful chemicals including phthalates, formaldehyde, certain PFAS chemicals, and more.
 Prohibits the sale and manufacture of cosmetics with certain harmful chemicals including phthalates, formaldehyde, certain PFAS chemicals, and more.
Illinois 2025 Adopted H.B.2516
Adopted
Prohibits the sale or distribution of certain products containing intentionally added PFAS including cookware, cosmetics, dental floss, juvenile products, menstrual products, intimate apparel, or food packaging or food contact products.
 Prohibits the sale or distribution of certain products containing intentionally added PFAS including cookware, cosmetics, dental floss, juvenile products, menstrual products, intimate apparel, or food packaging or food contact products.
Illinois 2025 Introduced H.B.1295
Introduced
Requires disclosure of products containing intentionally added PFAS. Prohibits intentionally added PFAS in certain products such as carpets, cookware, cosmetics, and food packaging. Prohibits the sale of any product with intentionally added PFAS unless deemed currently unavoidable by the Pollution Control Board.
 Requires disclosure of products containing intentionally added PFAS. Prohibits intentionally added PFAS in certain products such as carpets, cookware, cosmetics, and food packaging. Prohibits the sale of any product with intentionally added PFAS unless deemed currently unavoidable by the Pollution Control Board.
Iowa 2025 Introduced H.F.588
Introduced
Prohibits the use, manufacture, distribution, and sale of consumer products containing PFAS substances including carpets/rgs, cleaning products, cookware, cosmetics, dental loss, fabric treatments, juvenile products, menstruation products, textile furnishings, ski wax, and upholstered furniture. Prohibits the the sale of any product with intentionally added PFAS unless deemed an unavoidable use. Requires manufacturers to submit detailed information about products containing intentionally added PFAS.
 Prohibits the use, manufacture, distribution, and sale of consumer products containing PFAS substances including carpets/rgs, cleaning products, cookware, cosmetics, dental loss, fabric treatments, juvenile products, menstruation products, textile furnishings, ski wax, and upholstered furniture. Prohibits the the sale of any product with intentionally added PFAS unless deemed an unavoidable use. Requires manufacturers to submit detailed information about products containing intentionally added PFAS.
Maine 2023 Adopted L.D.1537
Adopted
Amends existing PFAS law. Beginning January 1, 2026, prohibits PFAS in cleaning products, cookware, cosmetics, dental floss, juvenile products, menstrual products, textile articles, ski wax, and upholstered furniture. Beginning January 1, 2029, prohibits PFAS in artificial turf, outdoor apparel for extreme weather conditions, and certain refrigerants. Requires disclosure by manufacturers who seek an exemption from the ban on PFAS in all products beginning in 2032 unless they ask for a currently unavoidable use designation.
 Amends existing PFAS law. Beginning January 1, 2026, prohibits PFAS in cleaning products, cookware, cosmetics, dental floss, juvenile products, menstrual products, textile articles, ski wax, and upholstered furniture. Beginning January 1, 2029, prohibits PFAS in artificial turf, outdoor apparel for extreme weather conditions, and certain refrigerants. Requires disclosure by manufacturers who seek an exemption from the ban on PFAS in all products beginning in 2032 unless they ask for a currently unavoidable use designation.
Maine 2021 Adopted LD 1503
Adopted
Requires manufacturers to report products containing intentionally added PFAS beginning in 2023. Prohibits the sale of residential carpets or rugs, and fabric treatments, that contain intentionally added PFAS beginning in 2023. Prohibits the sale of products containing intentionally added PFAS beginning in 2030 unless the use of PFAS in a product is specifically designated as “currently unavoidable”.
 Requires manufacturers to report products containing intentionally added PFAS beginning in 2023. Prohibits the sale of residential carpets or rugs, and fabric treatments, that contain intentionally added PFAS beginning in 2023. Prohibits the sale of products containing intentionally added PFAS beginning in 2030 unless the use of PFAS in a product is specifically designated as “currently unavoidable”.
Maryland 2026 Introduced S.B.686
Introduced
Prohibits the manufacture, sale, or distribution of certain products containing intentionally added PFAS, including, cleaning products, cookware, cosmetics and personal care products, feminine hygiene products, pet food package, juvenile products, intimacy products, fabric treatments, ski wax, textiles, furnishings, and paint. Requires state department to identify currently unavoidable uses of PFAS.
 Prohibits the manufacture, sale, or distribution of certain products containing intentionally added PFAS, including, cleaning products, cookware, cosmetics and personal care products, feminine hygiene products, pet food package, juvenile products, intimacy products, fabric treatments, ski wax, textiles, furnishings, and paint. Requires state department to identify currently unavoidable uses of PFAS.
Maryland 2026 Introduced S.B.656
Introduced
Prohibits certain intentionally added ingredients in cosmetics including phthalates, formaldehyde, mercury, parabens, certain dyes, and various PFAS.
 Prohibits certain intentionally added ingredients in cosmetics including phthalates, formaldehyde, mercury, parabens, certain dyes, and various PFAS.
Maryland 2026 Introduced H.B.1533
Introduced
Provides that a person who violates the prohibition on the manufacture, or sale of a cosmetic product that contains certain toxic chemicals is liable for certain civil damages.
 Provides that a person who violates the prohibition on the manufacture, or sale of a cosmetic product that contains certain toxic chemicals is liable for certain civil damages.
Maryland 2026 Introduced H.B.1022
Introduced
Prohibits the manufacture, sale, or distribution of certain products containing intentionally added PFAS including cleaning products, cookware, cosmetics, personal care products, feminine hygiene products, pet food packaging, juvenile products, intimacy products, fabric treatments, ski wax, textiles, textile furnishings, upholstered furniture, or paint.
 Prohibits the manufacture, sale, or distribution of certain products containing intentionally added PFAS including cleaning products, cookware, cosmetics, personal care products, feminine hygiene products, pet food packaging, juvenile products, intimacy products, fabric treatments, ski wax, textiles, textile furnishings, upholstered furniture, or paint.
Maryland 2021 Adopted HB 0643
Adopted
Bans 13 different types of PFAS, 2 types of phthalates, mercury, formaldehyde, and other chemicals of concern in the manufacture, sale, and delivery of cosmetics.
 Bans 13 different types of PFAS, 2 types of phthalates, mercury, formaldehyde, and other chemicals of concern in the manufacture, sale, and delivery of cosmetics.
Massachusetts 2025 Introduced S.1504
Introduced
Establishes a PFAS Remediation Trust Fund to support remediation efforts and regulatory compliance. Prohibits PFAS in consumer products, food packaging, and firefighting personal protective equipment and requires manufacturers to report products containing PFAS. Requires the Department of Environmental Protection to create regulations to phase out the use of sludge and to monitor PFAS in ambient air.
 Establishes a PFAS Remediation Trust Fund to support remediation efforts and regulatory compliance. Prohibits PFAS in consumer products, food packaging, and firefighting personal protective equipment and requires manufacturers to report products containing PFAS. Requires the Department of Environmental Protection to create regulations to phase out the use of sludge and to monitor PFAS in ambient air.
Massachusetts 2025 Introduced H2450/H4870
Introduced
Establishes a PFAS Remediation Trust Fund to support remediation efforts and regulatory compliance. Prohibits PFAS in consumer products, food packaging, and firefighting foam and requires manufacturers to report products containing PFAS. Requires the Department of Environmental Protection to create regulations to phase out the use of sludge and to monitor PFAS in ambient air.
 Establishes a PFAS Remediation Trust Fund to support remediation efforts and regulatory compliance. Prohibits PFAS in consumer products, food packaging, and firefighting foam and requires manufacturers to report products containing PFAS. Requires the Department of Environmental Protection to create regulations to phase out the use of sludge and to monitor PFAS in ambient air.
Minnesota 2023 Adopted H.F.2310
Adopted
Appropriates funding to address PFAS contamination. Restricts all unnecessary PFAS uses by 2032, bans 13 product categories specific uses, and requires companies to disclose presence of PFAS in any product. Prohibits the sale, distribution, and use of class B firefighting foam containing PFAS chemicals. Requires Pollution Control Agency to establish water quality standards for certain PFAS chemicals.
 Appropriates funding to address PFAS contamination. Restricts all unnecessary PFAS uses by 2032, bans 13 product categories specific uses, and requires companies to disclose presence of PFAS in any product. Prohibits the sale, distribution, and use of class B firefighting foam containing PFAS chemicals. Requires Pollution Control Agency to establish water quality standards for certain PFAS chemicals.
Missouri 2026 Introduced H.B.2400
Introduced
Prohibits the sale or distribution of products containing intentionally added PFAS including carpets, cleaning products, cookware, cosmetics, dental floss, fabric treatments, juvenile products, menstruation products, textile furnishings, ski wax, and upholstered furniture. By January 1, 2033, any product containing intentionally added PFAS will be banned unless deemed unavoidable
 Prohibits the sale or distribution of products containing intentionally added PFAS including carpets, cleaning products, cookware, cosmetics, dental floss, fabric treatments, juvenile products, menstruation products, textile furnishings, ski wax, and upholstered furniture. By January 1, 2033, any product containing intentionally added PFAS will be banned unless deemed unavoidable
New Jersey 2026 Introduced S.B.2266
Introduced
Prohibits the sale or distribution of menstrual products containing PFAS.
 Prohibits the sale or distribution of menstrual products containing PFAS.
New Jersey 2026 Adopted S.B.1042
Adopted
Prohibits intentionally added PFAS in cosmetics, carpet and fabric treatments, food packaging, and cookware. Requires manufacturers to label certain products containing PFAS.
 Prohibits intentionally added PFAS in cosmetics, carpet and fabric treatments, food packaging, and cookware. Requires manufacturers to label certain products containing PFAS.
New Jersey 2026 Introduced A.B.3125
Introduced
Prohibits menstrual products with intentionally added PFAS. Requires manufacturers to test their products for PFAS.
 Prohibits menstrual products with intentionally added PFAS. Requires manufacturers to test their products for PFAS.
New York 2025 Introduced S.3205
Introduced
Prohibits the sale of cosmetic and personal care products with intentionally added PFAS.
 Prohibits the sale of cosmetic and personal care products with intentionally added PFAS.
New York 2025 Adopted A.B.1502 / S.B.1548
Adopted
Prohibits certain unsafe chemicals in menstrual products such as lead, mercury, formaldehyde, triclosan, phthalates, parabens, and PFAS.
 Prohibits certain unsafe chemicals in menstrual products such as lead, mercury, formaldehyde, triclosan, phthalates, parabens, and PFAS.
New York 2025 Introduced A.1635
Introduced
Prohibits the sale of cosmetic and personal care products with intentionally added PFAS substances.
 Prohibits the sale of cosmetic and personal care products with intentionally added PFAS substances.
New York 2025 Introduced A 8585
Introduced
Prohibits intentionally added PFAS in consumer products including carpets, cookware, cosmetics, fabric treatments, and personal care items.
 Prohibits intentionally added PFAS in consumer products including carpets, cookware, cosmetics, fabric treatments, and personal care items.
North Carolina 2025 Introduced H.B.686
Introduced
Prohibits the sale of cosmetics with certain restricted substances including PFAS, heavy metals, parabens, phthalates, formaldehyde, benzophenones, asbestos, butylated compounds, siloxanes, phenylenediamines, and nitrosamines.
 Prohibits the sale of cosmetics with certain restricted substances including PFAS, heavy metals, parabens, phthalates, formaldehyde, benzophenones, asbestos, butylated compounds, siloxanes, phenylenediamines, and nitrosamines.
Ohio 2026 Introduced H.B.743
Introduced
Prohibits intentionally added PFAS in consumer products including carpets, cleaning products, cookware, cosmetics, juvenile items, and upholstered furniture. Requires manufacturers to submit detailed information about PFAS content, purposes, and testing results.
 Prohibits intentionally added PFAS in consumer products including carpets, cleaning products, cookware, cosmetics, juvenile items, and upholstered furniture. Requires manufacturers to submit detailed information about PFAS content, purposes, and testing results.
Oregon 2023 Adopted S.B.546
Adopted
Requires Oregon Health Authority to adopt and maintain list of designated high priority chemicals of concern used in cosmetic products and to periodically review and revise list. Requires manufacturers of cosmetic products sold in state to include on manufacturer’s website notice of certain chemicals used in products, beginning on January 1, 2027. Bans manufacture, sale and distribution of cosmetic products containing certain chemicals and classes of chemicals in state, beginning on January 1, 2027.
 Requires Oregon Health Authority to adopt and maintain list of designated high priority chemicals of concern used in cosmetic products and to periodically review and revise list. Requires manufacturers of cosmetic products sold in state to include on manufacturer’s website notice of certain chemicals used in products, beginning on January 1, 2027. Bans manufacture, sale and distribution of cosmetic products containing certain chemicals and classes of chemicals in state, beginning on January 1, 2027.
Pennsylvania 2026 Introduced H.B.2145
Introduced
Prohibits the use of PFAS in certain consumer products including cosmetics, dental floss, juvenile products, and menstrual products.
 Prohibits the use of PFAS in certain consumer products including cosmetics, dental floss, juvenile products, and menstrual products.
Rhode Island 2024 Adopted S.B.2152
Adopted
Prohibits intentionally added PFAS in certain products including artificial turf, carpets or rugs, cookware, cosmetics, fabric treatments, juvenile products, menstrual products, ski wax, and textile articles. Prohibits the manufacture, sale, or use of firefighting foam containing PFAS. Requires disclosure of PFAS in firefighting personal protective equipment.
 Prohibits intentionally added PFAS in certain products including artificial turf, carpets or rugs, cookware, cosmetics, fabric treatments, juvenile products, menstrual products, ski wax, and textile articles. Prohibits the manufacture, sale, or use of firefighting foam containing PFAS. Requires disclosure of PFAS in firefighting personal protective equipment.
Utah 2026 Introduced H.B.442
Introduced
Requires manufacturers of menstrual and feminine hygiene products to disclose certain chemicals and intentionally added ingredients on product packaging, including substances such as lead, cadmium, arsenic, and PFAS.
 Requires manufacturers of menstrual and feminine hygiene products to disclose certain chemicals and intentionally added ingredients on product packaging, including substances such as lead, cadmium, arsenic, and PFAS.
Vermont 2025 Adopted H.238
Adopted
Prohibits the manufacture, sale, or distribution for sale of consumer products containing PFAS including firefighting foam, artificial turf, carpets or rugs, cleaning products, cookware, cosmetic products, cookware, dental loss, food packaging, incontinence products, juvenile products, menstrual products, ski wax, and upholstered furniture.
 Prohibits the manufacture, sale, or distribution for sale of consumer products containing PFAS including firefighting foam, artificial turf, carpets or rugs, cleaning products, cookware, cosmetic products, cookware, dental loss, food packaging, incontinence products, juvenile products, menstrual products, ski wax, and upholstered furniture.
Vermont 2024 Adopted S.25
Adopted
Prohibit the manufacture, sale, and distribution of cosmetic and menstrual products containing certain chemicals and chemical classes. Prohibits the manufacture, sale, and distribution in Vermont of textiles containing perfluoroalkyl and polyfluoroalkyl substances. Prohibits the installationof any new athletic turf field containing perfluoroalkyl and polyfluoroalkyl substances.
 Prohibit the manufacture, sale, and distribution of cosmetic and menstrual products containing certain chemicals and chemical classes. Prohibits the manufacture, sale, and distribution in Vermont of textiles containing perfluoroalkyl and polyfluoroalkyl substances. Prohibits the installationof any new athletic turf field containing perfluoroalkyl and polyfluoroalkyl substances.
Virginia 2026 Introduced H.B.864
Introduced
Prohibits the manufacture and sale of cosmetic products containing certain toxic chemicals, including phthalates, formaldehyde, mercury, certain PFAS, and more.
 Prohibits the manufacture and sale of cosmetic products containing certain toxic chemicals, including phthalates, formaldehyde, mercury, certain PFAS, and more.
Virginia 2026 Adopted H.B.122
Adopted
Prohibits the manufacture, sale, or delivery of cosmetic products containing certain ingredients including phthalates, formaldehyde, mercury, certain PFAS, and more.
 Prohibits the manufacture, sale, or delivery of cosmetic products containing certain ingredients including phthalates, formaldehyde, mercury, certain PFAS, and more.
Washington 2023 Adopted H.B. 1047
Adopted
Beginning January 1, 2025, no person may manufacture, knowingly sell, offer for sale, distribute for sale, or distribute for use in this state any cosmetic product that contains any of the following intentionally added chemicals or chemical classes: ortho-phthalates, perfluoroalkyl and polyfluoroalkyl substances, formaldehyde, mercury, triclosan, and more.
 Beginning January 1, 2025, no person may manufacture, knowingly sell, offer for sale, distribute for sale, or distribute for use in this state any cosmetic product that contains any of the following intentionally added chemicals or chemical classes: ortho-phthalates, perfluoroalkyl and polyfluoroalkyl substances, formaldehyde, mercury, triclosan, and more.
Washington 2022 Adopted HB 1694
Adopted
Improves logistical processes for the regulation of priority chemicals in consumer products.
 Improves logistical processes for the regulation of priority chemicals in consumer products.
Wisconsin 2026 Introduced A.B.1191
Introduced
Prohibits intentionally added PFAS in consumer products including food packaging, carpets and rugs, cleaning products, cookware, cosmetics, dental floss, fabric treatments, juvenile products, menstruation products, textile furnishings, ski wax, and upholstered furniture. Requires manufacturers to submit detailed information about PFAS content, purposes, and testing results.
 Prohibits intentionally added PFAS in consumer products including food packaging, carpets and rugs, cleaning products, cookware, cosmetics, dental floss, fabric treatments, juvenile products, menstruation products, textile furnishings, ski wax, and upholstered furniture. Requires manufacturers to submit detailed information about PFAS content, purposes, and testing results.
Plastics 19 bills
State Year Status Bill Notes
California 2021 Adopted SB 343
Adopted
Requires the recyclability of a product or packaging to be truthful. Manufacturers would no longer be allowed to put the “chasing arrows” recycling symbol on items that aren’t actually recyclable. Products that contain PFAS chemicals can’t be labeled as recyclable.
 Requires the recyclability of a product or packaging to be truthful. Manufacturers would no longer be allowed to put the “chasing arrows” recycling symbol on items that aren’t actually recyclable. Products that contain PFAS chemicals can’t be labeled as recyclable.
Connecticut 2026 Introduced H.B.5524
Introduced
Prohibits the use of polystyrene containers by restaurants and the provision of single-use food service items unless requested by a consumer. Prohibits the distribution of single-use straws that contain PFAS.
 Prohibits the use of polystyrene containers by restaurants and the provision of single-use food service items unless requested by a consumer. Prohibits the distribution of single-use straws that contain PFAS.
Georgia 2026 Introduced H.B.1237
Introduced
Establishes producer responsibility regulations. Includes restrictions on certain addtives in packaging including lead, cadmium, mercury, PFAS, and phthalates.
 Establishes producer responsibility regulations. Includes restrictions on certain addtives in packaging including lead, cadmium, mercury, PFAS, and phthalates.
Hawaii 2026 Introduced H.B.644
Introduced
Prohibits the use, sale, or distribution of disposable or single-use plastic food ware and beverage service items, including those containing PFAS.
 Prohibits the use, sale, or distribution of disposable or single-use plastic food ware and beverage service items, including those containing PFAS.
Illinois 2026 Introduced S.B.3681
Introduced
Requires monitoring and reporting on microplastics and PFAS in Lake Michigan and the Illinois drinking water system.
 Requires monitoring and reporting on microplastics and PFAS in Lake Michigan and the Illinois drinking water system.
Massachusetts 2025 Introduced S.2187
Introduced
Prohibits state and municipal funding for artificial turf fields containing zinc, plastic, or intentionally-added PFAS.
 Prohibits state and municipal funding for artificial turf fields containing zinc, plastic, or intentionally-added PFAS.
Massachusetts 2025 Introduced H.926
Introduced
Establishes a Packaging Reduction and Recycling Program. Prohibits toxic substances in packaging, including phthalates, bisphenols, PFAS, lead, cadmium, mercury, halogenated flame retardants, and more. Prohibits packaging containing polyvinyl chloride, polystyrene, or polycarbonate.
 Establishes a Packaging Reduction and Recycling Program. Prohibits toxic substances in packaging, including phthalates, bisphenols, PFAS, lead, cadmium, mercury, halogenated flame retardants, and more. Prohibits packaging containing polyvinyl chloride, polystyrene, or polycarbonate.
Massachusetts 2025 Introduced H.916
Introduced
Prohibits the use of foam and solid polystyrene in disposable food service ware and packing materials. Restricts the provision of single-use disposable products. Sets a timeline for the removal of toxic substances from beverage containers, food ware, and food packaging
 Prohibits the use of foam and solid polystyrene in disposable food service ware and packing materials. Restricts the provision of single-use disposable products. Sets a timeline for the removal of toxic substances from beverage containers, food ware, and food packaging
Massachusetts 2025 Introduced H.911
Introduced
Requires truth in labeling of recycable and compostable products and packaging.
 Requires truth in labeling of recycable and compostable products and packaging.
New Jersey 2026 Introduced S.B.673
Introduced
Packaging reduction bill that includes a prohibition on the sale of packaging that contains certain substances, including toxic metals, PFAS, and vinyl chloride.
 Packaging reduction bill that includes a prohibition on the sale of packaging that contains certain substances, including toxic metals, PFAS, and vinyl chloride.
New Jersey 2026 Introduced S.B.670
Introduced
Requires producers of plastic packaging and certain other plastic products to reduce quantity of plastic sold. Prohibits the sale of packaging or products containing specific toxic substances, including lead, cadmium, mercury, hexavalent chromium, certain PFAS, ortho-phthalates, and bisphenols.
 Requires producers of plastic packaging and certain other plastic products to reduce quantity of plastic sold. Prohibits the sale of packaging or products containing specific toxic substances, including lead, cadmium, mercury, hexavalent chromium, certain PFAS, ortho-phthalates, and bisphenols.
New York 2025 Introduced S.1464
Introduced
Require companies selling or distributing packaging materials and products to register with a packaging reduction organization to develop a packaging reduction and recycling plan. Prohibits toxic substances in packaging, including ortho-phthalates, bisphenols, PFAS, heavy meals, halogenated flame retardants, formaldehyde, polyvinyl chloride, polystyrene, and more.
 Require companies selling or distributing packaging materials and products to register with a packaging reduction organization to develop a packaging reduction and recycling plan. Prohibits toxic substances in packaging, including ortho-phthalates, bisphenols, PFAS, heavy meals, halogenated flame retardants, formaldehyde, polyvinyl chloride, polystyrene, and more.
New York 2025 Introduced A.6671
Introduced
Requires truthful labeling of plastic recyclability. Products containing PFAS will not be considered recyclable.
 Requires truthful labeling of plastic recyclability. Products containing PFAS will not be considered recyclable.
New York 2025 Introduced A.1749
Introduced
Require companies selling or distributing packaging materials and products to register with a packaging reduction organization to develop a packaging reduction and recycling plan. Prohibits toxic substances in packaging, including ortho-phthalates, bisphenols, PFAS, heavy meals, halogenated flame retardants, formaldehyde, polyvinyl chloride, polystyrene, and more.

Prohibits toxic substances in packaging, including ortho-phthalates, bisphenols, PFAS, heavy meals, halogenated flame retardants, formaldehyde, polyvinyl chloride, polystyrene, and more.
North Carolina 2025 Introduced H882
Introduced
Establishes extended producer responsibility for packaging materials, prohibits certain toxic substances in packaging, and prohibit intentionally added PFAS in covered products.
 Establishes extended producer responsibility for packaging materials, prohibits certain toxic substances in packaging, and prohibit intentionally added PFAS in covered products.
Oregon 2023 Adopted S.B.543
Adopted
Prohibits food vendor from using polystyrene foam container in sales of prepared food. Prohibits person from selling or distributing foodware containers containing perfluoroalkyl or polyfluoroalkyl substances.
 Prohibits food vendor from using polystyrene foam container in sales of prepared food. Prohibits person from selling or distributing foodware containers containing perfluoroalkyl or polyfluoroalkyl substances.
Vermont 2022 Adopted H 446
Adopted
Requires state department to conduct a study on microplastics and PFAS in food packaging and food waste.
 Requires state department to conduct a study on microplastics and PFAS in food packaging and food waste.
Wisconsin 2026 Introduced S.B.778
Introduced
Establishes a comprehensive packaging reduction and recycling program. Requires producers to reduce packaging. Prohibits the use of certain toxic substances in packaging including phthalates, bisphenols, PFAS, halogenated flame retardants, formaldehyde, and more.

Wisconsin 2026 Introduced S.B.1138
Introduced
Prohibits intentionally added PFAS in certain products including food packaging, cleaning products, cosmetics, and textile furnishings. Allows the Department of Natural Resources to require manufacturers to test certain products for PFAS.

Require companies selling or distributing packaging materials and products to register with a packaging reduction organization to develop a packaging reduction and recycling plan. Prohibits toxic substances in packaging, including ortho-phthalates, bisphenols, PFAS, heavy meals, halogenated flame retardants, formaldehyde, polyvinyl chloride, polystyrene, and more.
 Require companies selling or distributing packaging materials and products to register with a packaging reduction organization to develop a packaging reduction and recycling plan. Prohibits toxic substances in packaging, including ortho-phthalates, bisphenols, PFAS, heavy meals, halogenated flame retardants, formaldehyde, polyvinyl chloride, polystyrene, and more.
New York 2025 Introduced A.1749
Introduced
Require companies selling or distributing packaging materials and products to register with a packaging reduction organization to develop a packaging reduction and recycling plan. Prohibits toxic substances in packaging, including ortho-phthalates, bisphenols, PFAS, heavy meals, halogenated flame retardants, formaldehyde, polyvinyl chloride, polystyrene, and more.
 Require companies selling or distributing packaging materials and products to register with a packaging reduction organization to develop a packaging reduction and recycling plan. Prohibits toxic substances in packaging, including ortho-phthalates, bisphenols, PFAS, heavy meals, halogenated flame retardants, formaldehyde, polyvinyl chloride, polystyrene, and more.
North Carolina 2025 Introduced H882
Introduced
Establishes extended producer responsibility for packaging materials, prohibits certain toxic substances in packaging, and prohibit intentionally added PFAS in covered products.
 Establishes extended producer responsibility for packaging materials, prohibits certain toxic substances in packaging, and prohibit intentionally added PFAS in covered products.
Oregon 2023 Adopted S.B.543
Adopted
Prohibits food vendor from using polystyrene foam container in sales of prepared food. Prohibits person from selling or distributing foodware containers containing perfluoroalkyl or polyfluoroalkyl substances.
 Prohibits food vendor from using polystyrene foam container in sales of prepared food. Prohibits person from selling or distributing foodware containers containing perfluoroalkyl or polyfluoroalkyl substances.
Wisconsin 2026 Introduced S.B.778
Introduced
Establishes a comprehensive packaging reduction and recycling program. Requires producers to reduce packaging. Prohibits the use of certain toxic substances in packaging including phthalates, bisphenols, PFAS, halogenated flame retardants, formaldehyde, and more.
 Establishes a comprehensive packaging reduction and recycling program. Requires producers to reduce packaging. Prohibits the use of certain toxic substances in packaging including phthalates, bisphenols, PFAS, halogenated flame retardants, formaldehyde, and more.
Textiles 34 bills
State Year Status Bill Notes
California 2022 Adopted AB 1817
Adopted
Restricts PFAS in apparel (both indoor and outdoor), accessories, and handbags starting in 2025. PFAS in outdoor apparel for severe wet conditions will be eliminated as of 2028 and manufacturers will be required to disclose the presence of PFAS in the products as of 2025. The law also directs manufacturers to use the least toxic alternative as they move away from PFAS.
 Restricts PFAS in apparel (both indoor and outdoor), accessories, and handbags starting in 2025. PFAS in outdoor apparel for severe wet conditions will be eliminated as of 2028 and manufacturers will be required to disclose the presence of PFAS in the products as of 2025. The law also directs manufacturers to use the least toxic alternative as they move away from PFAS.
Colorado 2024 Adopted S.B.24-081
Adopted
Prohibits the sale or distribution of certain outdoor apparel intended for extreme or extended use in severe wet conditions that contain intentionally added PFAS chemicals. Prohibits PFAS in cleaning products, cookware, dental floss, menstruation products, ski wax, and textile articles. Prohibits a person from installing artificial turf that contains intentionally added PFAS chemicals on state property.
 Prohibits the sale or distribution of certain outdoor apparel intended for extreme or extended use in severe wet conditions that contain intentionally added PFAS chemicals. Prohibits PFAS in cleaning products, cookware, dental floss, menstruation products, ski wax, and textile articles. Prohibits a person from installing artificial turf that contains intentionally added PFAS chemicals on state property.
Colorado 2022 Adopted HB 1345
Adopted
Restricts the sale and distribution of consumer products containing PFAS chemicals. Consumer products include oil and gas products; carpets or rugs; cosmetics; fabric treatments; food packaging; juvenile products; textile furnishings; and upholstered furniture.
 Restricts the sale and distribution of consumer products containing PFAS chemicals. Consumer products include oil and gas products; carpets or rugs; cosmetics; fabric treatments; food packaging; juvenile products; textile furnishings; and upholstered furniture.
Connecticut 2024 Adopted S.B.292
Adopted
Prohibits the sale or distribution of the following products containing intentionally added PFAS: apparel, carpets or rugs, cleaning products, cookware, cosmetics, dental floss, fabric treatments, children’s products, menstruation products, textile furnishings, ski wax, and upholstered furniture. Requires manufacturers to submit information on products containing intentionally added PFAS to the state.
 Prohibits the sale or distribution of the following products containing intentionally added PFAS: apparel, carpets or rugs, cleaning products, cookware, cosmetics, dental floss, fabric treatments, children’s products, menstruation products, textile furnishings, ski wax, and upholstered furniture. Requires manufacturers to submit information on products containing intentionally added PFAS to the state.
Illinois 2025 Introduced S.B.0117
Introduced
Prohibits certain products containing intentionally added PFAS, including cookware, cosmetics, dental floss, juvenile products, menstrual products, intimate apparel, and food packaging or food contact products.
 Prohibits certain products containing intentionally added PFAS, including cookware, cosmetics, dental floss, juvenile products, menstrual products, intimate apparel, and food packaging or food contact products.
Illinois 2025 Adopted H.B.2516
Adopted
Prohibits the sale or distribution of certain products containing intentionally added PFAS including cookware, cosmetics, dental floss, juvenile products, menstrual products, intimate apparel, or food packaging or food contact products.
 Prohibits the sale or distribution of certain products containing intentionally added PFAS including cookware, cosmetics, dental floss, juvenile products, menstrual products, intimate apparel, or food packaging or food contact products.
Illinois 2025 Introduced H.B.1295
Introduced
Requires disclosure of products containing intentionally added PFAS. Prohibits intentionally added PFAS in certain products such as carpets, cookware, cosmetics, and food packaging. Prohibits the sale of any product with intentionally added PFAS unless deemed currently unavoidable by the Pollution Control Board.
 Requires disclosure of products containing intentionally added PFAS. Prohibits intentionally added PFAS in certain products such as carpets, cookware, cosmetics, and food packaging. Prohibits the sale of any product with intentionally added PFAS unless deemed currently unavoidable by the Pollution Control Board.
Iowa 2025 Introduced H.F.588
Introduced
Prohibits the use, manufacture, distribution, and sale of consumer products containing PFAS substances including carpets/rgs, cleaning products, cookware, cosmetics, dental loss, fabric treatments, juvenile products, menstruation products, textile furnishings, ski wax, and upholstered furniture. Prohibits the the sale of any product with intentionally added PFAS unless deemed an unavoidable use. Requires manufacturers to submit detailed information about products containing intentionally added PFAS.
 Prohibits the use, manufacture, distribution, and sale of consumer products containing PFAS substances including carpets/rgs, cleaning products, cookware, cosmetics, dental loss, fabric treatments, juvenile products, menstruation products, textile furnishings, ski wax, and upholstered furniture. Prohibits the the sale of any product with intentionally added PFAS unless deemed an unavoidable use. Requires manufacturers to submit detailed information about products containing intentionally added PFAS.
Maine 2023 Adopted L.D.1537
Adopted
Amends existing PFAS law. Beginning January 1, 2026, prohibits PFAS in cleaning products, cookware, cosmetics, dental floss, juvenile products, menstrual products, textile articles, ski wax, and upholstered furniture. Beginning January 1, 2029, prohibits PFAS in artificial turf, outdoor apparel for extreme weather conditions, and certain refrigerants. Requires disclosure by manufacturers who seek an exemption from the ban on PFAS in all products beginning in 2032 unless they ask for a currently unavoidable use designation.
 Amends existing PFAS law. Beginning January 1, 2026, prohibits PFAS in cleaning products, cookware, cosmetics, dental floss, juvenile products, menstrual products, textile articles, ski wax, and upholstered furniture. Beginning January 1, 2029, prohibits PFAS in artificial turf, outdoor apparel for extreme weather conditions, and certain refrigerants. Requires disclosure by manufacturers who seek an exemption from the ban on PFAS in all products beginning in 2032 unless they ask for a currently unavoidable use designation.
Maine 2021 Adopted LD 1503
Adopted
Requires manufacturers to report products containing intentionally added PFAS beginning in 2023. Prohibits the sale of residential carpets or rugs, and fabric treatments, that contain intentionally added PFAS beginning in 2023. Prohibits the sale of products containing intentionally added PFAS beginning in 2030 unless the use of PFAS in a product is specifically designated as “currently unavoidable”.
 Requires manufacturers to report products containing intentionally added PFAS beginning in 2023. Prohibits the sale of residential carpets or rugs, and fabric treatments, that contain intentionally added PFAS beginning in 2023. Prohibits the sale of products containing intentionally added PFAS beginning in 2030 unless the use of PFAS in a product is specifically designated as “currently unavoidable”.
Maryland 2026 Introduced S.B.686
Introduced
Prohibits the manufacture, sale, or distribution of certain products containing intentionally added PFAS, including, cleaning products, cookware, cosmetics and personal care products, feminine hygiene products, pet food package, juvenile products, intimacy products, fabric treatments, ski wax, textiles, furnishings, and paint. Requires state department to identify currently unavoidable uses of PFAS.
 Prohibits the manufacture, sale, or distribution of certain products containing intentionally added PFAS, including, cleaning products, cookware, cosmetics and personal care products, feminine hygiene products, pet food package, juvenile products, intimacy products, fabric treatments, ski wax, textiles, furnishings, and paint. Requires state department to identify currently unavoidable uses of PFAS.
Maryland 2026 Introduced H.B.1022
Introduced
Prohibits the manufacture, sale, or distribution of certain products containing intentionally added PFAS including cleaning products, cookware, cosmetics, personal care products, feminine hygiene products, pet food packaging, juvenile products, intimacy products, fabric treatments, ski wax, textiles, textile furnishings, upholstered furniture, or paint.
 Prohibits the manufacture, sale, or distribution of certain products containing intentionally added PFAS including cleaning products, cookware, cosmetics, personal care products, feminine hygiene products, pet food packaging, juvenile products, intimacy products, fabric treatments, ski wax, textiles, textile furnishings, upholstered furniture, or paint.
Maryland 2022 Adopted HB 0275
Adopted
Restricts the use of PFAS chemicals in food packaging, rugs and carpets, and replaces PFAS-laden firefighting foam with safer alternatives. Requires notification for firefighter turnout gear that contains PFAS and stops the landfilling and incineration of PFAS foam.
 Restricts the use of PFAS chemicals in food packaging, rugs and carpets, and replaces PFAS-laden firefighting foam with safer alternatives. Requires notification for firefighter turnout gear that contains PFAS and stops the landfilling and incineration of PFAS foam.
Massachusetts 2025 Introduced S.1504
Introduced
Establishes a PFAS Remediation Trust Fund to support remediation efforts and regulatory compliance. Prohibits PFAS in consumer products, food packaging, and firefighting personal protective equipment and requires manufacturers to report products containing PFAS. Requires the Department of Environmental Protection to create regulations to phase out the use of sludge and to monitor PFAS in ambient air.
 Establishes a PFAS Remediation Trust Fund to support remediation efforts and regulatory compliance. Prohibits PFAS in consumer products, food packaging, and firefighting personal protective equipment and requires manufacturers to report products containing PFAS. Requires the Department of Environmental Protection to create regulations to phase out the use of sludge and to monitor PFAS in ambient air.
Massachusetts 2025 Introduced H2450/H4870
Introduced
Establishes a PFAS Remediation Trust Fund to support remediation efforts and regulatory compliance. Prohibits PFAS in consumer products, food packaging, and firefighting foam and requires manufacturers to report products containing PFAS. Requires the Department of Environmental Protection to create regulations to phase out the use of sludge and to monitor PFAS in ambient air.
 Establishes a PFAS Remediation Trust Fund to support remediation efforts and regulatory compliance. Prohibits PFAS in consumer products, food packaging, and firefighting foam and requires manufacturers to report products containing PFAS. Requires the Department of Environmental Protection to create regulations to phase out the use of sludge and to monitor PFAS in ambient air.
Minnesota 2023 Adopted H.F.2310
Adopted
Appropriates funding to address PFAS contamination. Restricts all unnecessary PFAS uses by 2032, bans 13 product categories specific uses, and requires companies to disclose presence of PFAS in any product. Prohibits the sale, distribution, and use of class B firefighting foam containing PFAS chemicals. Requires Pollution Control Agency to establish water quality standards for certain PFAS chemicals.
 Appropriates funding to address PFAS contamination. Restricts all unnecessary PFAS uses by 2032, bans 13 product categories specific uses, and requires companies to disclose presence of PFAS in any product. Prohibits the sale, distribution, and use of class B firefighting foam containing PFAS chemicals. Requires Pollution Control Agency to establish water quality standards for certain PFAS chemicals.
Minnesota 2019 Adopted HF 359
Adopted
Bans manufacture and sale of halogenated, phosphorus-based, nitrogen-based, and nanoscale flame retardants in residential upholstered furniture, children’s products, and residential and business textiles. Prohibits the manufacture and sale of PFAS-containing firefighting foam.
 Bans manufacture and sale of halogenated, phosphorus-based, nitrogen-based, and nanoscale flame retardants in residential upholstered furniture, children’s products, and residential and business textiles. Prohibits the manufacture and sale of PFAS-containing firefighting foam.
Missouri 2026 Introduced H.B.2400
Introduced
Prohibits the sale or distribution of products containing intentionally added PFAS including carpets, cleaning products, cookware, cosmetics, dental floss, fabric treatments, juvenile products, menstruation products, textile furnishings, ski wax, and upholstered furniture. By January 1, 2033, any product containing intentionally added PFAS will be banned unless deemed unavoidable
 Prohibits the sale or distribution of products containing intentionally added PFAS including carpets, cleaning products, cookware, cosmetics, dental floss, fabric treatments, juvenile products, menstruation products, textile furnishings, ski wax, and upholstered furniture. By January 1, 2033, any product containing intentionally added PFAS will be banned unless deemed unavoidable
Nevada 2021 Adopted AB 97
Adopted
Prohibits the use of firefighting foam containing PFAS; establishes standards for the disposal of firefighting foam containing PFAS; establishes a working group to study issues related to PFAS and develop recommendations for state action; prohibits any flame retardant organohalogenated chemical in the manufacture, sale, and distribution of children’s products, upholstered residential furniture, residential textiles, business textiles, or mattresses (specifically exempts polymeric flame retardants).
 Prohibits the use of firefighting foam containing PFAS; establishes standards for the disposal of firefighting foam containing PFAS; establishes a working group to study issues related to PFAS and develop recommendations for state action; prohibits any flame retardant organohalogenated chemical in the manufacture, sale, and distribution of children’s products, upholstered residential furniture, residential textiles, business textiles, or mattresses (specifically exempts polymeric flame retardants).
New Jersey 2026 Introduced S.B.1281
Introduced
Prohibits sale, manufacture, and distribution of certain apparel containing intentionally added PFAS.
 Prohibits sale, manufacture, and distribution of certain apparel containing intentionally added PFAS.
New Jersey 2026 Adopted S.B.1042
Adopted
Prohibits intentionally added PFAS in cosmetics, carpet and fabric treatments, food packaging, and cookware. Requires manufacturers to label certain products containing PFAS.
 Prohibits intentionally added PFAS in cosmetics, carpet and fabric treatments, food packaging, and cookware. Requires manufacturers to label certain products containing PFAS.
New York 2026 Introduced S.B.9073
Introduced
Prohibits the sale of certain products that contain PFAS, including air care products, automotive products, cleaning products, polish or floor maintenance products, cookware, fabric treatment, rugs, ski wax, textiles and textile articles. Requires manufacturers of products containing PFAS to provide notice.
 Prohibits the sale of certain products that contain PFAS, including air care products, automotive products, cleaning products, polish or floor maintenance products, cookware, fabric treatment, rugs, ski wax, textiles and textile articles. Requires manufacturers of products containing PFAS to provide notice.
New York 2025 Introduced A.7738
Introduced
Prohibits the sale of consumer and household products containing PFAS, including textile articles, rugs, fabric treatments, cookware, ski waxes, architectural paints, cleaning products, and dental floss.
 Prohibits the sale of consumer and household products containing PFAS, including textile articles, rugs, fabric treatments, cookware, ski waxes, architectural paints, cleaning products, and dental floss.
New York 2025 Introduced A 8585
Introduced
Prohibits intentionally added PFAS in consumer products including carpets, cookware, cosmetics, fabric treatments, and personal care items.
 Prohibits intentionally added PFAS in consumer products including carpets, cookware, cosmetics, fabric treatments, and personal care items.
New York 2022 Adopted S.1322
Adopted
Prohibits the use of perfluoroalkyl and polyfluoroalkyl substances in apparel as an intentionally added chemical.
 Prohibits the use of perfluoroalkyl and polyfluoroalkyl substances in apparel as an intentionally added chemical.
New York 2022 Adopted A 09279
Adopted
Establishes a carpet collection program. Beginning December 31, 2024, prohibits the sale of carpets containing perfluoroalkyl and polyfluoroalkyl substances.
 Establishes a carpet collection program. Beginning December 31, 2024, prohibits the sale of carpets containing perfluoroalkyl and polyfluoroalkyl substances.
Ohio 2026 Introduced H.B.743
Introduced
Prohibits intentionally added PFAS in consumer products including carpets, cleaning products, cookware, cosmetics, juvenile items, and upholstered furniture. Requires manufacturers to submit detailed information about PFAS content, purposes, and testing results.
 Prohibits intentionally added PFAS in consumer products including carpets, cleaning products, cookware, cosmetics, juvenile items, and upholstered furniture. Requires manufacturers to submit detailed information about PFAS content, purposes, and testing results.
Rhode Island 2024 Adopted S.B.2152
Adopted
Prohibits intentionally added PFAS in certain products including artificial turf, carpets or rugs, cookware, cosmetics, fabric treatments, juvenile products, menstrual products, ski wax, and textile articles. Prohibits the manufacture, sale, or use of firefighting foam containing PFAS. Requires disclosure of PFAS in firefighting personal protective equipment.
 Prohibits intentionally added PFAS in certain products including artificial turf, carpets or rugs, cookware, cosmetics, fabric treatments, juvenile products, menstrual products, ski wax, and textile articles. Prohibits the manufacture, sale, or use of firefighting foam containing PFAS. Requires disclosure of PFAS in firefighting personal protective equipment.
Vermont 2025 Adopted H.238
Adopted
Prohibits the manufacture, sale, or distribution for sale of consumer products containing PFAS including firefighting foam, artificial turf, carpets or rugs, cleaning products, cookware, cosmetic products, cookware, dental loss, food packaging, incontinence products, juvenile products, menstrual products, ski wax, and upholstered furniture.
 Prohibits the manufacture, sale, or distribution for sale of consumer products containing PFAS including firefighting foam, artificial turf, carpets or rugs, cleaning products, cookware, cosmetic products, cookware, dental loss, food packaging, incontinence products, juvenile products, menstrual products, ski wax, and upholstered furniture.
Vermont 2024 Adopted S.25
Adopted
Prohibit the manufacture, sale, and distribution of cosmetic and menstrual products containing certain chemicals and chemical classes. Prohibits the manufacture, sale, and distribution in Vermont of textiles containing perfluoroalkyl and polyfluoroalkyl substances. Prohibits the installationof any new athletic turf field containing perfluoroalkyl and polyfluoroalkyl substances.
 Prohibit the manufacture, sale, and distribution of cosmetic and menstrual products containing certain chemicals and chemical classes. Prohibits the manufacture, sale, and distribution in Vermont of textiles containing perfluoroalkyl and polyfluoroalkyl substances. Prohibits the installationof any new athletic turf field containing perfluoroalkyl and polyfluoroalkyl substances.
Vermont 2021 Adopted S 20
Adopted
Prohibits the manufacture, sale, distribution, and use of firefighting foam containing intentionally added PFAS beginning October 1, 2023 . Requires manufacturers selling firefighting equipment to disclose if it contains PFAS. Prohibits the manufacture, sale, and distribution of food packaging containing intentionally added PFAS, inks, dyes, pigments, adhesives, stabilizers, coatings, plasticizers, or any other additives to which ortho-phthalates have been intentionally added. Allows the Department of Health to adopt rules prohibiting bisphenols in food packaging. Prohibits PFAS in carpets, rugs, fabric treatments, and ski wax.
 Prohibits the manufacture, sale, distribution, and use of firefighting foam containing intentionally added PFAS beginning October 1, 2023 . Requires manufacturers selling firefighting equipment to disclose if it contains PFAS. Prohibits the manufacture, sale, and distribution of food packaging containing intentionally added PFAS, inks, dyes, pigments, adhesives, stabilizers, coatings, plasticizers, or any other additives to which ortho-phthalates have been intentionally added. Allows the Department of Health to adopt rules prohibiting bisphenols in food packaging. Prohibits PFAS in carpets, rugs, fabric treatments, and ski wax.
Washington 2022 Adopted HB 1694
Adopted
Improves logistical processes for the regulation of priority chemicals in consumer products.
 Improves logistical processes for the regulation of priority chemicals in consumer products.
Wisconsin 2026 Introduced S.B.1138
Introduced
Prohibits intentionally added PFAS in certain products including food packaging, cleaning products, cosmetics, and textile furnishings. Allows the Department of Natural Resources to require manufacturers to test certain products for PFAS.

 Prohibits intentionally added PFAS in consumer products including food packaging, carpets and rugs, cleaning products, cookware, cosmetics, dental floss, fabric treatments, juvenile products, menstruation products, textile furnishings, ski wax, and upholstered furniture. Alaska 2026 Introduced S.B.219
Introduced
Requires annual testing of drinking water from all public water systems for PFAS.
 Requires annual testing of drinking water from all public water systems for PFAS.
Alaska 2026 Introduced H.B.235
Introduced
Requires annual testing of drinking water from all public water systems for PFAS. Businesses involved in the production, use, or disposal of PFAS may face potential liability costs.
 Requires annual testing of drinking water from all public water systems for PFAS. Businesses involved in the production, use, or disposal of PFAS may face potential liability costs.
Arizona 2026 Introduced H.B.2546
Introduced
Allocates $1 million for a health study on PFAS levels in blood from contaminated drinking water sources.

Introduced
Establishes maximum contaminant levels for certain PFAS and other pollutants in drinking water.

Introduced
Authorizes the California State Water Resources Control Board to fund projects that address PFAS in drinking water and source water.

Adopted

Requires public water systems to monitor for PFAS.

Introduced
Authorizes bonds to fund remediation of wells contaminated by PFAS in Franklin.

Introduced
Requires testing of PFAS substances in drinking water.
 Requires testing of PFAS substances in drinking water.
Connecticut 2021 Adopted HB 6690
Adopted
Provides up to $1,150,000 in grants-in-aid to municipalities for the purpose of providing potable water and for assessment and remedial action to address PFAS pollution. Effective July 1, 2021.

Connecticut 2021 Adopted HB 6666
Adopted
Requires water bottler companies to test the water source they are bottling for PFAS. Effective January 1, 2022.
 Requires water bottler companies to test the water source they are bottling for PFAS. Effective January 1, 2022.
Delaware 2025 Adopted S.B.72
Adopted
Requires the Division of Public Health to create a website where Delaware residents can find out the level of PFAS in their public drinking water systems. Requires the Division to notify public water utilities if the PFAS in their water exceeds certain limits.
 Requires the Division of Public Health to create a website where Delaware residents can find out the level of PFAS in their public drinking water systems. Requires the Division to notify public water utilities if the PFAS in their water exceeds certain limits.

Adopted
Directs the Department of Natural Resources and Environmental Control and the Division of Public Health to establish maximum contaminant levels for PFOA and PFOS.
 Directs the Department of Natural Resources and Environmental Control and the Division of Public Health to establish maximum contaminant levels for PFOA and PFOS.

Adopted
Requires the Department of Environmental Protection to adopt statewide rules for cleanup target levels for perfluoroalkyl and polyfluoroalkyl substances in soil and groundwater by a specified date.

Introduced

Introduced

 Requires publicly owned treatment works to collect information from industrial users about PFAS discharges.

Introduced

Introduced
Establishes a committee to monitor and recommend policies for PFAS mitigation in wastewater systems, holding manufacturers responsible for costs.

Indiana 2026 Introduced H.B.1239
Introduced
Requires the Indiana department of health to establish state maximum contaminant levels for PFAS in water.
 Requires the Indiana department of health to establish state maximum contaminant levels for PFAS in water.
Indiana 2026 Introduced H.B.1110
Introduced
Requires the department of environmental management to adopt maximum contaminant levels for PFAS chemicals and effluent limitation standards for wastewater containing PFAS chemicals by July 1, 2027. Establishes the PFAS chemical testing and remediation fund. Creates a PFAS chemical grant program.
 Requires the department of environmental management to adopt maximum contaminant levels for PFAS chemicals and effluent limitation standards for wastewater containing PFAS chemicals by July 1, 2027. Establishes the PFAS chemical testing and remediation fund. Creates a PFAS chemical grant program.
Maine 2026 Introduced L.D.2115
Introduced

Adopted

Introduced

Adopted

Adopted
Establishes regulations concerning the management and testing of landfill leachate for PFAS chemicals.
